FULL CONTENT:
# What are the Impacts and Benefits of Cyber-physical Systems?

![](https://blog.engineering.vanderbilt.edu/hubfs/Blog-Images/Smart%20transportation%20system.jpg)

Cyber-physical systems technology seeks to develop the processes, networking and technology needed for the seamless integration of cyber and physical systems, but the **benefits of cyber-physical systems** **are not limited** to fields related to technology and engineering.

*So, what are the practical impacts and benefits of cyber-physical systems?*

The science of **CPS has the capability to impact technology in a wide variety of industries and organizations**, and CPS allows us to imagine, create, develop, refine and perpetuate smart systems in fields that result in the betterment of industries, communities and individuals.

[![CPS eBook thumbnail-1](https://blog.engineering.vanderbilt.edu/hs-fs/hubfs/CPS%20eBook%20thumbnail-1.png?width=305&name=CPS%20eBook%20thumbnail-1.png)](https://info.engineering.vanderbilt.edu/everything-you-need-to-know-about-a-master-of-engineering-in-cyber-physical-systems)

![CPS eBook thumbnail-1](https://blog.engineering.vanderbilt.edu/hs-fs/hubfs/CPS%20eBook%20thumbnail-1.png?width=305&name=CPS%20eBook%20thumbnail-1.png)

[![Download Vanderbilt University’s cyber-physical systems guide to learn more  about obtaining a graduate degree in engineering that will prepare you to  transform the CPS field.](https://no-cache.hubspot.com/cta/default/6893998/414d846e-e1a8-4027-9f13-a93da5e24153.png)](https://cta-redirect.hubspot.com/cta/redirect/6893998/414d846e-e1a8-4027-9f13-a93da5e24153)

![Download Vanderbilt University’s cyber-physical systems guide to learn more  about obtaining a graduate degree in engineering that will prepare you to  transform the CPS field.](https://no-cache.hubspot.com/cta/default/6893998/414d846e-e1a8-4027-9f13-a93da5e24153.png)

Regarding the benefits of cyber-physical systems technology, according to the [Information Technology Laboratory](https://www.nist.gov/itl/ssd/cyber-physical-systems): “The ability to design and build successful cyber-physical systems will address many national priorities in ways that traditional computer science cannot in areas as diverse as **aerospace, automotive, energy, disaster response, health care, manufacturing and city management**. Standards, protocols and test methods that support the discovery, interoperability and composition of components used to build these cyber physical systems will promote innovation, improve economic viability at the same time allowing systems to become more efficient and reduce resource-use.”

In other words, the immeasurable benefits of cyber-physical systems is the acceleration of technological progress — progress that positively impacts countless fields, organizations and ultimately, the lives of others.

## **Understanding the impact and benefits of cyber-physical systems technology across diverse industries:**

Here are just a few ways in which the [benefits of CPS technology](/3-benefits-of-cyber-physical-systems-in-an-uncertain-world) have impacted different industries:

### **Smart city management**

[TechTarget](http://internetofthingsagenda.techtarget.com/definition/smart-city) defines a smart city as “a municipality that uses information and communication technologies to increase operational efficiency, share information with the public and improve both the quality of government services and citizen welfare.” A smart city ecosystem is complex, including systems related to intelligent traffic management, emergency response technologies and public safety solutions — the use of cyber-physical systems technology is paramount in planning, implementing and improving the optimization of smart cities.

### Infrastructure

In 2021, the United States’ infrastructure received a C- rating from the [American Society of Civil Engineers’ Report Card for America’s Infrastructure](https://infrastructurereportcard.org/).  
  
What is the solution to our unstable infrastructure? [Improving infrastructure starts with technology.](https://blog.engineering.vanderbilt.edu/civil-engineers-improve-infrastructure-sustainability-using-smart-construction-management-heres-how) Using advanced digital technologies like IoT sensors and video cameras, smart infrastructure empower smart cities to enhance the experience of citizens, businesses and city operators.  
  
CPS engineers must master cutting-edge technologies in order to upgrade existing city infrastructure systems — many of which have not been updated in years. Bringing novel technologies to physical frameworks allows for [more upgrades to critical infrastructure](https://techcrunch.com/2020/11/30/the-road-to-smart-city-infrastructure-starts-with-research/).

### **Automotive**

IoT and CPS technology have made specific advancements in smart car technologies that can make vehicle transportation safer; “[blind-spot monitoring, lane-departure warning and forward collision warning](https://www.machinedesign.com/markets/automotive/article/21838119/will-smart-car-technologies-save-money)” are just three features that, if implemented in all cars in the United States, could reduce the number of crashes and in turn, save millions of dollars a year.

[![smart-city-visualization-1-1](https://blog.engineering.vanderbilt.edu/hs-fs/hubfs/smart-city-visualization-1-1.jpg?width=500&name=smart-city-visualization-1-1.jpg)](https://info.engineering.vanderbilt.edu/everything-you-need-to-know-about-a-master-of-engineering-in-cyber-physical-systems)

![smart-city-visualization-1-1](https://blog.engineering.vanderbilt.edu/hs-fs/hubfs/smart-city-visualization-1-1.jpg?width=500&name=smart-city-visualization-1-1.jpg)

### **Agriculture**

Sometimes referred to as smart agriculture or digital farming, CPS-related technology has resulted in advancements that help drive efficiencies on farms: from drones and satellites that relay images related to plant health to smart sensors on tractors or harvesters that provide information on soil type and condition.

### **Sustainability**

Society continues to seek more solutions to today’s overwhelming need for sustainable practices in business, health care and countless other industries, and CPS technology often makes the advancement of these solutions possible — solutions such as [public electric transport, affordable energy storage, accessible solar power and clean recycling initiatives](https://www.mckinsey.com/business-functions/sustainability/our-insights/sustainability-blog/these-9-technological-innovations-will-shape-the-sustainability-agenda-in-2019) are a direct result of IoT and CPS technology.

### **Security**

The emergence of smart technology has [increased security measures](https://medium.com/@indianappd/innovative-smart-security-technologies-most-impressive-examples-91e7b68d3d4f) in a variety of ways. “From mobile app development for real-time remote monitoring to fully fledged intelligent surveillance systems,” CPS technology has empowered smart security to advance and improve.

### **Health care**

CPS technology has made a [multitude of medical advancements](https://econsultancy.com/internet-of-things-healthcare/) possible. From a smart monitoring system that tracks cancer patients’ response to treatment to a smart continuous tool that sends data on glucose levels to the wearer’s smartphone, the field of health care has greatly improved as a result of CPS science and technology.

## **Why Vanderbilt University is a strategic choice when seeking to develop skills in cyber-physical systems:**

A global leader in cyber-physical systems research and education, Vanderbilt University is uniquely qualified to offer a well-recognized, cross-disciplinary [master of engineering in cyber-physical systems](https://engineering.vanderbilt.edu/academics/m_eng/CPS/index.php).

In fact, V**anderbilt faculty significantly contributed to the formation of the CPS research agenda and are leaders in the CPS community.**

Further, Vanderbilt is a national and international leader in CPS research, receiving substantial research funding in CPS and making a significant impact in both academic and industrial R&D. CPS-related research at Vanderbilt has been truly interdisciplinary, integrating efforts across multiple application domains (defense, transportation, energy and health care).

Vanderbilt also hosts the [CPS Virtual Organization](http://cps-vo.org/) (CPS-VO) – the national hub for CPS research.

## **Transform your future** **—** **and the lives of others** **—** **with a master of engineering in cyber-physical systems:**

*“I grew significant knowledge of the vast domain space, acquired disciplined work ethic and project leadership skills. The master of engineering in cyber-physical systems provided me with respect in the field and significantly opened up future career opportunities.”* [***— Ron Picard, M.Eng. 2019***](/why-i-joined-the-cyber-physical-systems-community-at-vanderbilt-university)

At Vanderbilt University School of Engineering, the [master of engineering in cyber-physical systems](https://engineering.vanderbilt.edu/academics/m_eng/CPS/index.php) is a 12-month, 30-hour interdisciplinary program that aims to put graduates on a fast career path in companies of all sizes and in a variety of industries that are critical to U.S. innovation and competitiveness.

If you are interested in joining the diverse and inclusive Vanderbilt engineering community, we encourage you to [request more information](https://info.engineering.vanderbilt.edu/school-of-engineering-request-more-information) or [start your online application](https://engineering.vanderbilt.edu/admissions/) today.

[**![CPS eBook thumbnail](https://blog.engineering.vanderbilt.edu/hs-fs/hubfs/CPS%20eBook%20thumbnail.png?width=305&name=CPS%20eBook%20thumbnail.png)**](https://info.engineering.vanderbilt.edu/everything-you-need-to-know-about-a-master-of-engineering-in-cyber-physical-systems)

![CPS eBook thumbnail](https://blog.engineering.vanderbilt.edu/hs-fs/hubfs/CPS%20eBook%20thumbnail.png?width=305&name=CPS%20eBook%20thumbnail.png)

--- SOURCE 4: Cyber-physical systems: definition, components, and security ---
URL: https://newrelic.com/blog/best-practices/cyber-physical-systems

SUMMARY:
Cyber-Physical Systems integrate digital and physical processes across industries. Learn how CPS enhances security, efficiency, and monitoring

FULL CONTENT:
# Cyber-physical systems: definition, components, and security

![Michael McPherson](/sites/default/files/styles/1x1_100w/public/2024-05/McPhersonHeadshot.jpeg?h=5ca34a59&itok=-GLkdQvx)

By [Michael McPherson, Principal Product Marketing Manager Security](/blog/authors/michael-mcpherson)

Cyber-physical systems (CPS) fuse the digital and physical worlds by integrating sensing, computation, control, and networking into physical objects. These systems enable machines to directly interact with their environments, leading to new innovations across the manufacturing, healthcare, transportation, and energy sectors. This nascent, interdisciplinary niche—rooted in embedded systems but blazing entirely new trails—shows immense promise beyond how it’s utilized today.

Accordingly, the global CPS market continues to expand at a rapid pace across industries as more companies adopt these technologies to enhance automation and efficiency. From smart manufacturing facilities to autonomous transportation, CPS represents a massive advancement in how we apply computing to real-world challenges. To properly leverage this emerging technology, engineers need to understand the many layers of CPS so they can develop both hardware and software that bridges digital intelligence and physical reality.

This guide walks you through key concepts in CPS, including components, networking, and common use cases. We also explore the multilayered challenge of CPS security, and the complementary role that artificial intelligence and blockchain-based technologies can play.

## Components of cyber-physical systems

CPS combines technologies into architectures that enable real-time interaction between computational and physical domains. Sensors and actuators form the **perception layer,** serving as the interface with the physical world by collecting data and executing commands. Computational elements like embedded systems and real-time processors act as the **control layer**. Communication infrastructure enables reliable data exchange between sensors, controllers, and actuators through specialized networking protocols. Taken together, these components can monitor, analyze, and manipulate physical environments with minimal human intervention.

### Sensors and actuators

Sensors provide the eyes and ears of cyber-physical systems, collecting vital data about physical conditions such as temperature, pressure, motion, and proximity. These components transform environmental properties into digital signals that can be analyzed by the computational core of the system. Actuators then complete the feedback loop by converting digital commands into physical actions such as movement or regulating physical processes.

Common sensors used in CPS include accelerometers and gyroscopes in autonomous vehicles, temperature and humidity sensors in smart buildings, and pressure sensors in industrial applications.

### Control systems and software

Control systems in CPS form the brain that processes sensor data and determines appropriate responses through complex algorithms and computational models. These systems typically implement real-time operating systems such as FreeRTOS or RTLinux that can handle time-critical operations with predictable response times so that physical processes remain coordinated and stable.

The software architecture in CPS often features multiple layers—from embedded firmware that directly interacts with hardware, to middleware that facilitates communication between components, to application-level software that implements decision logic. This layered approach helps CPS manage the complexity of integrating computational and physical processes.

### Networking and communication

CPS networking uses protocols like Profinet, EtherCAT, and MQTT for reliable data transmission between sensors, controllers, and actuators.

## Cyber-physical systems examples

Cyber-physical systems have been deployed across numerous industries, with organizations typically reporting significant improvements in operational efficiency, reduced downtime, and enhanced safety protocols. Implementations range from large-scale industrial applications to consumer-facing technologies, and all are transforming how people interact with machines. Here are some notable examples where CPS has had a substantial impact:

## Security in cyber-physical systems

Security is a key challenge for cyber-physical systems since vulnerabilities can impact both data and physical operations. Unlike IT systems, CPS security breaches can cause disrupted services, equipment damage, or safety threats. These systems often combine legacy operational technology with modern connected components, creating security gaps at integration points that attackers can potentially exploit through techniques like firmware manipulation or false data injection.

Because CPS security spans digital and physical domains, protection strategies must effectively address all layers while maintaining operational performance requirements. Organizations should implement comprehensive access controls, network segmentation, secure communication protocols, and continuous monitoring calibrated specifically for CPS environments. Security in cyber-physical systems demands specialized approaches that can evaluate the system without disrupting its operations, which often requires simulation environments that mirror production systems.

### Threats and risks to CPS

Cyber-physical systems face threats including conventional cyberattacks and specialized threats targeting the digital-physical interface. Adversaries can exploit vulnerabilities to gain unauthorized access, manipulate sensors, intercept communications, or inject false commands directly into control systems. The consequences extend beyond data loss to include production downtime, equipment failure, and environmental damage.

Safety-critical applications like medical devices and transportation systems present even greater risks, as a successful attack could directly endanger human lives. Vulnerable attack surfaces in CPS include wireless communications, remote access points, and supply chain components where security validation may be incomplete.

### Security strategies and best practices

Securing cyber-physical systems requires a defense-in-depth approach that addresses vulnerabilities across the entire system architecture. Effective strategies include implementing robust authentication mechanisms to verify device identities, encrypting communications to prevent eavesdropping and tampering, and deploying intrusion detection systems that can identify anomalous behavior in both network traffic and physical processes.

Organizations should adopt a lifecycle approach to CPS security, incorporating security requirements during system design, implementing secure coding practices during development, and maintaining vigilant monitoring during operations. Regular security assessments and incident response planning are essential components of a comprehensive CPS security program.

### Role of AI and blockchain in CPS security

Artificial intelligence provides many opportunities to improve CPS security through advanced behavioral analysis and threat detection. For example, machine learning algorithms can establish baselines of normal system behavior and then identify deviations that might indicate an attack, providing earlier warning of potential security breaches than traditional rule-based approaches. AI systems can also correlate data from diverse sensors and sources—consider a building management system, for instance, that combines data from access controls, network traffic, and physical sensors to identify coordinated intrusion attempts.

Blockchain technology also offers promising approaches to enhancing CPS security through distributed validation and tamper-evident record keeping. By creating immutable audit trails of system transactions and implementing decentralized authentication mechanisms, blockchain-based solutions can help protect the integrity of critical CPS components and reduce single points of failure in security architectures.

## Future of cyber-physical systems

The evolution of CPS is accelerating beyond conventional applications as quantum computing begins to enhance real-time processing capabilities and digital twin technology creates perfect virtual replicas of physical systems. Next-gen systems are poised to achieve new levels of autonomy through advanced reinforcement learning algorithms, allowing CPS to not only respond to environments but actually predict changes before they occur. For instance, the fusion of CPS with neuromorphic computing – a computing approach that mirrors the thought process of the human brain, using artificial “neurons” and receptors to process information – shows potential for delivering systems that can process sensory data in ways that mimic human perception for more intuitive machine-environment interactions.

Cross-domain CPS implementations could thoroughly redefine industry boundaries as transportation networks are able to communicate directly with energy grids and urban infrastructure, creating intelligent ecosystems that can optimize resource usage across entire cities. Factories will reconfigure production lines without human intervention; energy microgrids will anticipate and respond to demand fluctuations autonomously; and residential buildings will adapt to occupant behavior in real time. These developments are not without significant challenges—from privacy, to standardization, to cybersecurity, to resilience, and beyond—but the potential benefits are driving rapid advancement across sectors as wide-ranging as aerospace and defense to healthcare and consumer applications.

## Next steps

When your business relies on cyber-physical systems, blind spots aren’t just an inconvenience—they could be catastrophic. Imagine a manufacturing line suddenly halting because temperature sensors have drifted out of calibration, or smart grid components failing during peak demand. Without proper visibility, these problems can remain hidden until they trigger costly downtime or system failures. The complexity of CPS demands observability solutions that can track both digital signals and their physical impacts.

New Relic’s observability platform cuts through the complexity by capturing telemetry from every layer of your CPS infrastructure, from edge devices and control systems to [networking components and backend services](/platform/network-performance-monitoring). Engineers gain real-time insights into system behavior so they can detect anomalies before they lead to operational issues. The platform’s correlation capabilities connect seemingly unrelated events across the physical-digital boundary, revealing patterns that would otherwise remain invisible.

See how New Relic’s [**observability platform**](/platform) can be applied to cyber-physical systems.

--- SOURCE 5: CPSSEC - Cyber Physical Systems Security ---
URL: https://www.dhs.gov/archive/science-and-technology/cpssec

SUMMARY:
The Cyber Physical Systems Security (CPSSEC) project addresses security concerns for cyber physical systems (CPS) and internet of things (IoT) devices. Whether referencing the forward-collision prevention capability of a car, a medical device’s ability to adapt to circumstances in real-time or the latest IoT innovation, these systems are a source of competitive advantage in today’s innovation economy and provide vast opportunities for DHS and Homeland Security Enterprise missions. The Cyber Physical Systems Vision Statement from the Networking Information Technology Research and Development (NITRD) Program identifies nine areas of critical importance to government: agriculture, building controls, defense, energy, emergency response, health care, manufacturing and industry, society and transportation. The CPSSEC project currently is focused on security for automotive, medical devices and building controls, with an increasing interest in IoT security.

## Archived Content

# Cyber Physical Systems Security

![Cyber Physcial Systems Security: Building in Security](/sites/default/files/styles/webp_original_size/public/images/st/CSD/cps_lock_graphic_final_web.jpg.webp?itok=mqWEacYn)
The Cyber Physical Systems Security (CPSSEC) project addresses security concerns for cyber physical systems (CPS) and internet of things (IoT) devices. CPS and IoT play an increasingly important role in critical infrastructure, government and everyday life. Automobiles, medical devices, building controls and the smart grid are examples of CPS. Each includes smart networked systems with embedded sensors, processors and actuators that sense and interact with the physical world and support real-time, guaranteed performance in safety-critical applications. The closely related area of IoT continues to emerge and expand as costs drop and the confluence of sensors, platforms and networks increases. Whether referencing the forward-collision prevention capability of a car, a medical device’s ability to adapt to circumstances in real-time or the latest IoT innovation, these systems are a source of competitive advantage in today’s innovation economy and provide vast opportunities for DHS and Homeland Security Enterprise missions. At the same time, CPS and IoT also increase cybersecurity risks and attack surfaces. The consequences of unintentional faults or malicious attacks could have severe impact on human lives and the environment. Proactive and coordinated efforts are needed to strengthen security and reliance for CPS and IoT.

![Cyber Physcial Systems Security: Building in Security](/sites/default/files/styles/webp_original_size/public/images/st/CSD/cps_lock_graphic_final_web.jpg.webp?itok=mqWEacYn)

## Motivation

This is a critical time in the design and deployment of CPS and IoT. Advances in networking, computing, sensing and control systems have enabled a broad range of new devices. These systems are being designed and deployed now, however, security often is left for later. Industry is driven by functional requirements and fast-moving markets. Designs are evolving rapidly and standards are only now emerging. Many devices now being deployed have lifespans measured in decades, so current design choices will impact the next several decades in the transportation, health care, building controls, emergency response, energy and other sectors.

To understand the scope of the challenge, consider the recent advances in the cars we drive, the medical devices we depend on, the systems that operate our buildings, the power grid and a vast number of new IoT devices. Modern cars can automatically brake to avoid a collision, medical devices can monitor conditions in real-time and adapt to changes, and buildings and the energy grid are being enhanced with a number of new smart services. In fact, it is anticipated billions of new IoT devices will be connected to the internet. If security is overlooked, we run the risk of unintentional faults or malicious attacks changing how cars brake, how medical devices adapt and how buildings and the smart grid respond to events. Cybersecurity only becomes more challenging if billions of devices with security vulnerabilities are added. Addressing security issues by bolting solutions onto widely deployed systems is not viable. Security issues must be analyzed, understood and addressed in the early stages of design and deployment.

## Approach

The CPS and IoT space is vast and covers many distinct sectors. The Cyber Physical Systems Vision Statement from the [Networking Information Technology Research and Development](https://www.nitrd.gov/) (NITRD) Program identifies nine areas of critical importance to government: agriculture, building controls, defense, energy, emergency response, health care, manufacturing and industry, society and transportation. Further, these areas share crosscutting issues of cybersecurity, economics, interoperability, privacy, safety and reliability and social aspects. No single agency can tackle these areas alone.

The CPSSEC project is taking a layered approach to these challenges as illustrated in the CPS Security Pyramid. At the pyramid’s base, DHS is working with other agencies such as the [National Science Foundation](https://www.nsf.gov/) (NSF) to address fundamental and crosscutting challenges. The goal is to ensure the basic building blocks for CPS and IoT security are available and realistically feasible for use in specific systems.

At the core of the pyramid, DHS-funded applied R&D addresses sectors where S&T investments can have maximum impact. These areas are chosen based on a combination of impacts delivered to DHS’s homeland security mission, technical readiness and investments by other federal agencies that provide funding. The CPSSEC project currently is focused on security for automotive, medical devices and building controls, with an increasing interest in IoT security.

At the pyramid’s top, CPSSEC engages through a combination of coordination with the appropriate sector-specific oversight agency, government research agencies, industry engagement and support for sector-focused innovation, small business efforts and technology transition. This work encompasses the development of sector-specific industry consortiums.

## Performers

**[Adventium Labs](http://www.adventiumlabs.com): Intrinsically Secure, Open, and Safe Control of Essential LayErs (ISOSCELES)**  
The integration of medical devices with the internet has resulted in an expanding attack surface and vulnerabilities for hospitals and medical facilities. ISOSCELES provides a safe and secure platform where medical device companies can build their own medical device applications.

**[Arizona State University](http://www.asu.edu): A Verifiable Framework for Cyber Physical Attacks and Countermeasures in a Resilient Electric Power Grid**  
DHS recorded 161 cyberattacks on electric power systems in 2013 compared to 31 in 2011. Attackers only need to identify the weakest defense in an expanding attack surface. This effort provides modeling of sophisticated attacks in order better understand, mitigate, and recover by utilizing lessons learned from previous attacks.

**[Brigham Young University](http://www.byu.edu): Mission Impact Situational Awareness Toll for Distributed Operations Management of Cyber Physical Human Critical Infrastructures**   
This effort contributes to infrastructure security by developing a tool for mission-focused situational awareness and resiliency planning by building a core back-end software that automates vulnerability assessment of an infrastructure system, embeds the software in an application specific wrapper, and deploys this application specific prototype and validates its utility by collecting feedback from experts.

**[Circadence](http://www.circadence.com): SCADA Cyber Weakness in and Artificial Intelligence – Enabled Cyber Training Platform**  
This effort will use Project Ares, a game-based interface for immersive cybersecurity training, to develop a compelling supervisory control and data acquisition device training environment known as “Mission 8.” Mission 8 is a critical infrastructure cyber mission providing training opportunities for CI protection teams a different levels (easy, medium, and hard) and scoring for the following skillsets:

**[Constellation Software Engineering (CSE)](http://www.cse-corp.com): Security for the Internet of Things (IoTSEC)**  
This effort is conducting a survey consisting of identification of IoT devices, infrastructure support, and data flows in order to establish a security baseline and conduct a proof-of-concept demonstration that consolidates multiple sensor readings and outlines the protocols and security architectures involved. This initial footprint will provide answers to key questions surrounding potential cybersecurity issues while setting the stage for establishing new cyber protocols to help mitigate risk and assure operational integrity.

**[Department of Transportation Volpe Transportation Center](http://www.volpe.dot.gov): Joint Agency Work on Automotive Cyber Security**  
This effort promotes automotive cybersecurity best practices and guidelines in the private sector while working with industry on key challenges and pre-competitive research via the Automotive Cybersecurity Industry Consortium .

**[HRL Laboratories](http://www.hrl.com): Side-Channel Causal Analysis for Design of Cyber Physical Security**   
This effort’s novel approach of side-channel defense, is to secure vulnerabilities and causal inconsistencies in vehicle cyber physical systems. Advanced side-channel monitors will analyze signals, and causal couplings between cyber and physical realms that are beyond the control of an attacker.

**[Kansas State University](http://www.k-state.edu): Modeling Security/Safety Interactions for Buildings for Compositional Safety**  
This effort fundamentally changes the breach and patch cycle by anchoring security into embedded controller architectures and using real-world scenario analysis and modeling on various types of building automation systems.

**[Medical Device Innovation Safety and Security (MDISS)](https://mdiss.org/): Medical Device Risk Assessment Platform**  
Cyber vulnerabilities in medical devices and their risk controls are an asymmetric threat to patient safety, privacy and the usability of medical devices. MDISS provides health delivery organizations and medical device manufacturers a system to assess these cybersecurity risks. The platform allows the effective assessment of cyber risks and the implementation of appropriate policies and controls to mitigate risks.

**[New York University](http://www.nyu.edu): Securely Updating Automobiles**  
This effort is developing technology that will enhance the security of automobile update systems by adding and validating metadata to improve resilience to attacks. Deploying this technology will be a cost-effective method to mitigate the risk of cyberattacks on automobile software update infrastructure.

**[Pacific Northwest National Laboratories](http://www.pnnl.gov): Maritime Cybersecurity Review**  
A cyberattack within the maritime sector can result in the disruption of a variety of processes including, but not limited to, navigation, propulsion, entry requirements documentation and compliance, freight/container/cargo, management, traffic controls, and port security. This effort is intended to build upon the understanding of existing processes and key elements of maritime cargo and terminal operations as they relate to CPS security.

**[University of Michigan Ann Arbor](https://umich.edu) and [University of Illinois at Urbana-Champaign](https://illinois.edu/index.html): Support for Security and Safety of Programmable IoT Systems**  
This effort introduces novel techniques and system designs to prevent, limit, and contain risks that arise in programmable IoT ecosystems.

**[University of Michigan Transportation Research Institute (UMTRI)](http://www.umtri.umich.edu): Secure Software Update Over-the-Air for Ground Vehicles Specification and Prototype**     
The objective of this effort is to create an automotive standard for secure Software updates Over-the-Air (SOTA) and demonstrate a proof-of-concept integration in a vehicle.

enriched_points.expanded_content: CPS integrates real-time monitoring and automation, optimizing operations across industries like manufacturing and transportation, thus significantly enhancing overall system efficiency.
By improving resource management and minimizing downtime, CPS can lead to substantial cost savings in production and maintenance, directly impacting the bottom line.
CPS provides real-time data and predictive analytics, allowing for proactive safety measures to prevent accidents and ensure secure operations in critical environments.
CPS harnesses vast amounts of data to facilitate informed decision-making, driving strategic improvements and fostering innovation across various sectors.
